name: Manananggal
description: The Manananggal is a terrifying creature from Filipino folklore, a nocturnal, self-segmenting vampire-like being. It is depicted as an old woman capable of severing its upper torso from its lower body, sprouting bat-like wings, and flying off into the night in search of prey, typically pregnant women whose unborn children they crave. The term "Manananggal" comes from the Tagalog word "tanggal," which means to remove or separate. This refers to the creature's ability to detach its upper body. While the lower half remains standing, vulnerable, the upper half hunts, using its long, proboscis-like tongue to suck the blood and vital organs from its victims. They are said to favor rural areas and are often associated with dark, secluded places. Belief in the Manananggal is still prevalent in some parts of the Philippines, particularly in rural provinces. Protecting oneself involves using salt, garlic, or blessed oil. The creature is a powerful symbol of fear and the unknown in Filipino culture, embodying the dangers lurking in the shadows and the fragility of life, especially for expectant mothers. The Manananggal represents a chilling embodiment of the monstrous feminine, preying on the vulnerable and defying natural order.
subtitle: Self-Segmenting Vampire of the Night
appearance: During the day, the Manananggal appears as an ordinary woman, often described as shy or unassuming. At night, it separates from its lower body, growing large, bat-like wings. Its upper torso becomes grotesque, with sharp claws and a long, thin, black tongue used to suck blood. The exposed flesh is raw and dripping, and its eyes glow with a malevolent red light. The lower half of its body remains standing, appearing like a severed stump.
habitat: Rural areas, particularly in provinces of the Philippines. They are often found in dark, secluded places like forests, caves, or abandoned buildings. They prefer areas where they can easily target pregnant women and are rarely seen in bustling cities.
behavior: The Manananggal is a nocturnal predator, hunting for prey under the cover of darkness. It is cunning and stealthy, often disguising itself as an ordinary woman to lure victims. Once it finds a target, it uses its long tongue to drain the blood and organs, often targeting pregnant women and their unborn children. It is said to be territorial and protective of its hunting grounds. The Manananggal is motivated by hunger and a desire to inflict harm.

keyMyths.title: The Pregnant Woman's Nightmare
keyMyths.description: A pregnant woman unknowingly lives near a Manananggal. One night, the creature attacks, attempting to drain the life from her and her unborn child, but she is saved by a timely intervention of a family member who uses salt to ward off the monster.

keyMyths.title: The Secret of the Severed Body
keyMyths.description: A man falls in love with a woman, unaware she is a Manananggal. He discovers her secret and prevents her from rejoining her upper and lower body by sprinkling salt on the severed halves, causing her to die at sunrise.

sources.author: Maximo Ramos
sources.work: The Creatures of Philippine Lower Mythology
sources.description: A comprehensive study of various mythical creatures in Philippine folklore.
sources.text: A comprehensive study of various mythical creatures in Philippine folklore.

sources.author: Damiana Eugenio
sources.description: A collection of various folk tales, including stories about the Manananggal.
